title: TanStack CVE-2026-45321 GHSA-g7cv-rxg3-hmpx: TeamPCP Hijacks Legitimate Release Pipeline to Publish First SLSA-Attested Malicious npm Packages
description: On May 11 2026 between 19:20 and 19:26 UTC, TeamPCP published 84 malicious artifacts across 42 @tanstack namespace packages by hijacking TanStack's legitimate OIDC-based release pipeline mid-workflow using three chained vulnerabilities, producing the first documented malicious npm packages carrying valid SLSA provenance cryptographic attestation generated by Sigstore.
label: AV-410

title: TanStack Worm Self-Propagates to Mistral AI UiPath and 170 Additional npm Packages Within Hours of Initial Compromise
description: The TanStack supply chain worm self-propagated from the initial 42 @tanstack packages to @mistralai/mistralai, UiPath, and at least 170 additional npm packages within hours of the May 11 2026 compromise, with @tanstack/react-router alone receiving over 12.7 million weekly downloads amplifying the attack's blast radius massively.
label: AV-410

title: TeamPCP Compromises Aqua Security Trivy CVE-2026-33634 Supply Chain Attack Forces CISA KEV Catalog Addition
description: Between February 27 and March 22 2026, TeamPCP compromised the widely-used Aqua Security Trivy container scanner by gaining unauthorized repository access, pushing a malicious release through official channels, and force-pushing tags on trivy-action and setup-trivy, resulting in CVE-2026-33634 being added to CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog.
label: AV-410

title: Trivy Security Scanner Weaponized as Credential-Stealing Malware Delivery Mechanism Across Thousands of CI Workflows
description: TeamPCP weaponized the Trivy security scanner by compromising its official release to steal credentials from the CI pipelines where it ran, with the irony that a tool designed to find vulnerabilities became the delivery mechanism for credential-stealing malware executing with access to CI secrets across thousands of affected workflows.
label: AV-400

title: axios npm Package Compromised March 31 2026 Delivering RAT to macOS Windows and Linux via 100 Million Weekly Download Vector
description: The axios npm package with approximately 100 million weekly downloads was compromised on March 31 2026 when attackers published malicious versions that silently pulled in a hidden dependency deploying a Remote Access Trojan across macOS, Windows, and Linux systems, with Huntress observing at least 135 endpoints contacting attacker C2 infrastructure during the three-hour exposure window.
label: AV-400

title: axios Compromise Uses Stolen npm Publish Token After TeamPCP Trivy Attack in Coordinated March 2026 Supply Chain Campaign
description: The March 2026 axios npm compromise used stolen npm publish tokens obtained through the TeamPCP Trivy scanner attack earlier that month, demonstrating credential mutualization where secrets harvested from one supply chain attack directly enabled a subsequent attack against a different high-value package with 100 million weekly downloads.
label: AV-410

title: Bitwarden CLI npm Package CVE-2026-4.0 Compromised by TeamPCP April 22 2026 Between 5:57 and 7:30 PM EST
description: The @bitwarden/cli npm package version 2026.4.0 was compromised by TeamPCP between 5:57 PM and 7:30 PM EST on April 22 2026, with the Bitwarden security team confirming that while the CLI distribution mechanism was affected, no end user vault data was accessed or at risk during the 94-minute exposure window.
label: AV-400

title: LiteLLM PyPI Supply Chain Attack SNYK-PYTHON-LITELLM-15762713 Installs Multi-Stage Credential Stealer and Persistent Backdoor via .pth File
description: The LiteLLM PyPI supply chain attack documented as SNYK-PYTHON-LITELLM-15762713 involved tampered versions that embedded malicious code in the proxy server module executing on import, and a malicious .pth file executing automatically at Python interpreter startup requiring no explicit import, installing a multi-stage credential stealer and persistent backdoor targeting millions of daily downloads.
label: AV-400

title: Telnyx npm Package with Over One Million Monthly Downloads Compromised in Early 2026 Supply Chain Attack Wave
description: The Telnyx npm package with over one million monthly downloads was compromised as part of the early 2026 supply chain attack wave alongside Trivy, LiteLLM, and axios, demonstrating that attackers consistently targeted high-download packages to maximize the reach of credential theft and malware deployment across developer environments.
label: AV-400

title: SANDWORM_MODE Campaign Introduces Adaptive Supply Chain Worm Spreading Through Typosquatted npm Packages
description: The SANDWORM_MODE supply chain campaign introduced an adaptive worm that spread through typosquatted npm packages and compromised developer environments, representing a new variant of supply chain worm toolchain that adapted its propagation techniques based on the specific npm ecosystem configuration found on each victim system.
label: AV-200

title: React Native npm Packages Weaponized by GlassWorm Campaign Using Solana Blockchain as Decentralized C2 Channel
description: Compromised React Native npm packages were weaponized as part of the GlassWorm campaign, using the Solana blockchain as a decentralized command-and-control channel to steal developer credentials and cryptocurrency wallet data, blending trusted packages with decentralized infrastructure to evade traditional C2 detection and takedown measures.
label: AV-400

title: solana-py Malicious PyPI Package Exploits GitHub PyPI Naming Mismatch to Deceive Developers Using Hugging Face Spaces for Exfiltration
description: The malicious PyPI package solana-py exploited a naming mismatch between GitHub and PyPI repositories to deceive developers expecting the legitimate Solana Python API, stealing cryptocurrency wallet keys and using a Hugging Face Spaces URL as the data exfiltration endpoint to avoid traditional malicious domain detection.
label: AV-200

title: 281000 Bogus npm Packages with Latin Gibberish Names Published Overnight to Strain Registry Infrastructure and Overwhelm Security Scanning
description: Over 281,000 bogus npm packages with Latin gibberish names appeared in the npm registry overnight, mostly empty or forked from a blockchain-related GitHub repository without functional purpose, straining registry infrastructure and security scanning capabilities while potentially serving as a smokescreen for genuinely malicious package publications.
label: AV-200

title: netfetcher and pyfetcher PyPI Packages Pose as Windows Utilities to Download Malicious Executables Including Fake Netflix Checker
description: The malicious PyPI packages netfetcher and pyfetcher posed as legitimate Windows utility tools to download malicious executables including a fake Netflix account checker that went undetected by antivirus tools, exploiting user trust in utility packages to deliver malware that evaded traditional signature-based endpoint security.
label: AV-200

title: pac4j-jwt CVE-2026-29000 Critical Vulnerability Allows Token Forgery and Authentication Bypass Through Dependency Chain Propagation
description: The critical vulnerability CVE-2026-29000 in the pac4j-jwt security library allowed attackers to forge authentication tokens and bypass security controls, with researchers identifying the vulnerability's spread through dependency chains demonstrating how a single flaw in a widely-used security library can quietly propagate risk across an entire open source ecosystem.
label: AV-509

title: Sonatype Tracks 845000 Total Malicious Packages Across npm PyPI and Maven Central Marking 188 Percent Year-Over-Year Rise
description: Sonatype tracked 16,279 new malicious packages across npm, PyPI, and Maven Central in a single reporting period, pushing the cumulative total past 845,000 and marking a 188 percent year-over-year rise in malicious package volume, with over half targeting data exfiltration and data corruption incidents doubling to 3 percent.
label: AV-200

title: Lazarus Group Publishes 107 npm and PyPI Packages with 30000 Downloads Impersonating Developer Tools to Deploy Persistent Backdoors
description: Sonatype documented 234 malicious npm and PyPI packages linked to North Korea's Lazarus Group including a campaign of 107 packages receiving 30,000 downloads that mimicked trusted developer tools, stole credentials, profiled host systems, and deployed persistent backdoors potentially exposing over 36,000 victims to nation-state supply chain compromise.
label: AV-200

title: Shai-Hulud 2.0 pre-install Execution Dramatically Widens Impact Compared to postinstall and Introduces Home Directory Destruction Fallback
description: The Shai-Hulud 2.0 campaign introduced execution during pre-install rather than postinstall lifecycle hooks, dramatically widening attack impact across developer machines and CI/CD pipelines, while adding an aggressive fallback mechanism that attempts to securely overwrite and delete every writable file in the victim's home directory if credential exfiltration fails.
label: AV-410

title: Shai-Hulud 2.0 Affects 25000 Malicious GitHub Repositories Across 350 Unique Users with Repository Description Sha1-Hulud The Second Coming
description: The Shai-Hulud 2.0 November 2025 campaign affected over 25,000 malicious GitHub repositories created across approximately 350 unique compromised user accounts, with the distinctive repository description Sha1-Hulud: The Second Coming used to identify campaign artifacts and enabling security researchers to track and quantify the attack's full scope.
label: AV-410

title: Shai-Hulud 2.0 New Payload Files setup_bun.js and bun_environment.js Disguise Malware as Helpful Bun JavaScript Runtime Installer
description: The Shai-Hulud 2.0 campaign introduced new payload files named setup_bun.js and bun_environment.js that disguised the malware delivery mechanism as a helpful Bun JavaScript runtime installer, exploiting developer familiarity with legitimate Bun tooling to reduce suspicion when the malicious payload files were discovered in compromised environments.
label: AV-400

title: Shai-Hulud Third Coming April 22 2026 Wave Includes String Shai-Hulud The Third Coming in Compromised Repository Descriptions
description: Unit 42 documented a third Shai-Hulud campaign wave starting April 22 2026 identifiable by the string Shai-Hulud: The Third Coming in compromised repository descriptions, demonstrating the persistence and branding consistency of the supply chain worm campaign across multiple distinct attack waves targeting the npm ecosystem.
label: AV-410

title: npm Ecosystem September 2025 Reaches Critical Inflection Point as Shai-Hulud Marks End of Nuisance Era and Beginning of High-Consequence Threat Landscape
description: Unit 42 documented that the npm ecosystem reached a critical inflection point in September 2025 when the Shai-Hulud worm marked the end of the nuisance era of npm attacks and the beginning of a high-consequence threat landscape where systematic campaigns by threat actors weaponize the trust that powers modern software development.
label: AV-410

title: Infrastructure-Level CI/CD Persistence Emerges as New Supply Chain Attack Objective Beyond Simple Credential Theft
description: Unit 42 documented that modern supply chain attacks evolved beyond simple credential theft to pursue infrastructure-level persistence by embedding malicious code in CI/CD pipelines, using stolen secrets to maintain long-term undetectable access to enterprise environments rather than one-time credential exfiltration.
label: AV-410

title: Multi-Stage Sleeper npm Payloads Activate Only Under Specific Environmental Conditions to Evade Automated Registry Scanners
description: Unit 42 documented that post-Shai-Hulud supply chain attacks increasingly deploy dormant sleeper dependencies that only activate under specific environmental conditions such as CI runner detection or organization membership verification, allowing malicious packages to pass automated registry scanning before revealing their credential-stealing behavior.
label: AV-400

title: Shai-Hulud Proves npm Registry Can Be Used as Force Multiplier for Malware Distribution at Ecosystem Scale
description: Unit 42 analysis concluded that the Shai-Hulud worm proved the npm registry could be weaponized as a force multiplier for malware distribution at ecosystem scale, where a single compromised package automatically produces dozens of additional infected packages creating a self-sustaining infection cycle that overwhelms manual security response.
label: AV-509

title: Red Hat Confirms s1ngularity Shai-Hulud and Popular Packages Campaigns as Three Major npm Supply Chain Compromises Requiring Collective Response
description: Red Hat Product Security formally documented the s1ngularity, Shai-Hulud, and Popular Packages npm supply chain compromise campaigns, confirming all three attack campaigns were halted only through collective action by upstream projects, package ecosystems, security vendors, and distributors working together rather than any single organization's defensive response.
label: AV-410

title: Shai-Hulud CVE-2025-10894 CVE-2025-59037 CVE-2025-59140 Series Documents Worm Variants Across Multiple npm Package Compromises
description: Red Hat Product Security assigned multiple CVEs to Shai-Hulud worm variants including CVE-2025-10894, CVE-2025-59037, CVE-2025-59140, CVE-2025-59143, CVE-2025-59162, CVE-2025-59142, CVE-2025-59144, CVE-2025-59330, CVE-2025-59331, and CVE-2025-59141, documenting the scale of the supply chain compromise across the npm ecosystem.
label: AV-410

title: s1ngularity Nx Compromise Listed Alongside Shai-Hulud and Popular Packages as Three Distinct Major npm Supply Chain Attack Campaigns in 2025
description: Red Hat formally categorized s1ngularity targeting Nx and related plugins, Shai-Hulud self-propagating worm, and the Popular Packages campaign targeting high-profile npm maintainers maintaining the most frequently downloaded packages as three distinct major npm supply chain attack campaigns that collectively compromised hundreds of Node.js components in 2025.
label: AV-509

title: JFrog Documents New Compromised Packages in Largest npm Attack in History Affecting chalk debug and strip-ansi Simultaneously
description: JFrog identified new compromised packages in what it documented as the largest npm attack in history, with the Popular Packages campaign simultaneously compromising chalk, debug, strip-ansi, color-convert, wrap-ansi, and other foundational packages used by billions of JavaScript projects worldwide.
label: AV-200

title: Aikido Security Documents 20 Popular npm Packages Compromised Including chalk debug strip-ansi color-convert and wrap-ansi
description: Aikido Security documented 20 popular npm packages compromised in the Popular Packages campaign including chalk, debug, strip-ansi, color-convert, and wrap-ansi, with StepSecurity confirming the full list and Sonatype and JFrog providing additional analysis of the phishing attack vector used to compromise maintainer Qix's account.
label: AV-200

title: Trivy Incomplete Credential Rotation Enables TeamPCP Persistent Repository Access for 23 Days Before Malicious Release Publication
description: The TeamPCP Trivy compromise succeeded because incomplete credential rotation after initial unauthorized repository access allowed attackers to maintain persistent access for 23 days from February 27 to March 22 2026, demonstrating that incomplete credential rotation following a security incident creates windows for extended sophisticated supply chain attacks.
label: AV-410

title: Attackers Do Not Create Fake Packages in 2026 Campaigns Instead Gaining Unauthorized Access to Trusted Projects Through Stolen Credentials
description: Security researchers documented a fundamental shift in 2026 supply chain attack strategy where attackers stopped creating fake packages and instead gained unauthorized access to trusted projects through stolen credentials, with malicious versions appearing authentic because they moved through the same registries and CI/CD integrations teams rely on every day.
label: AV-200

title: TeamPCP Takes Public Credit for TanStack Bitwarden Trivy Checkmarx and SAP npm Supply Chain Attacks Across Multiple Months
description: The TeamPCP threat group publicly took credit for multiple high-profile supply chain attacks across several months including TanStack, Bitwarden CLI, Aqua Security Trivy, Checkmarx infrastructure, and SAP npm packages, establishing TeamPCP as the most prolific and publicly identified supply chain attack threat group in 2026.
label: AV-410

title: Shai-Hulud 2026 Campaigns Demonstrate Post-Shai-Hulud Era Where Each Wave Builds on Previous Technical Sophistication
description: Snyk documented that each Shai-Hulud campaign wave built on the previous wave's technical sophistication, with Wave 4 TanStack notable for achieving publication of malicious packages indistinguishable from legitimate ones by provenance attestation, a capability no prior supply chain attack had demonstrated, representing a new ceiling of supply chain attack sophistication.
label: AV-410

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Jest Test Configuration Files Alter Test Suite Behavior and Coverage Reporting
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Jest JavaScript testing configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter test suite inclusion patterns and coverage collection settings, causing CI/CD pipelines to skip security-critical test cases or report false coverage metrics while appearing to run complete test suites.
label: AV-300

title: LiteLLM Compromise Targets AI Developer Tooling as High-Value Supply Chain Vector for Cloud Credential and API Key Theft
description: The LiteLLM PyPI supply chain attack specifically targeted AI developer tooling as a high-value supply chain vector because LiteLLM users typically hold cloud provider credentials, LLM API keys for multiple services, and CI/CD pipeline secrets providing access to AI infrastructure costs valued at millions of dollars.
label: AV-400

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in ESLint Configuration Files Allow Hidden Disabling of Security-Critical Lint Rules
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in ESLint configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter rule severity settings, causing ESLint to silently disable security-critical rules that would catch injection vulnerabilities and authentication bypasses while appearing to enforce full rule compliance in configuration review.
label: AV-300

title: MAL-2026-3432 GHSA-3q49-cfcf-g5fm Confirms mistralai npm Package Infected as TanStack Worm Propagation Victim
description: The @mistralai/mistralai npm package was registered in the OSSF malicious packages database as MAL-2026-3432 with advisory GHSA-3q49-cfcf-g5fm, confirming infection through TanStack worm propagation and demonstrating that AI company npm packages became collateral supply chain attack victims through automated worm self-propagation mechanisms.
label: AV-509

title: GLPMC Tanstack-Worm-Detector and omarpr mini-shai-hulud-ioc-scanner Community Tools Enable Rapid Supply Chain Attack Detection
description: The supply chain security community responded to the TanStack and Mini Shai-Hulud attacks by publishing community-maintained detection scripts including GLPMC/Tanstack-Worm-Detector and omarpr/mini-shai-hulud-ioc-scanner, demonstrating that community-driven IoC scanning tools play a critical role in rapid supply chain attack detection and victim notification.
label: AV-509

title: TanStack Three Chained Vulnerabilities Required to Hijack Legitimate OIDC Release Pipeline Demonstrating Sophisticated Multi-Step Attack
description: The TanStack supply chain attack required chaining three distinct vulnerabilities to successfully hijack the legitimate OIDC-based release pipeline, demonstrating that sophisticated supply chain attackers conduct multi-step attack chains where no single vulnerability alone would have been sufficient to achieve malicious package publication with valid provenance attestation.
label: AV-410

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in vitest Configuration Files Alter Test Environment and Mock Function Behavior
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in vitest testing framework configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter test environment settings and mock function configurations, causing unit tests to run in different environments with different mock behaviors from what developers intend when reviewing test configuration files.
label: AV-300

title: Seven to Fourteen Day Dependency Cooldown Policy Demonstrated Effective Against 80 Percent of 2025 Major Supply Chain Attacks
description: Security researcher William Woodruff demonstrated that a 7 to 14 day dependency cooldown policy where organizations wait before accepting newly published package versions would have prevented approximately 80 percent of major 2025 supply chain attacks by creating a detection buffer during which security vendors identify and remove malicious packages before developer installation.
label: AV-509

title: All 2025 and 2026 Supply Chain Attack Campaigns Share Single Objective of Stealing Developer and CI/CD Pipeline Secrets
description: GitGuardian documented that despite different threat actor groups and targeting strategies, all major 2025 and 2026 npm, PyPI, and Docker Hub supply chain attack campaigns shared the single primary objective of stealing secrets from developer environments and CI/CD pipelines, including API keys, cloud credentials, SSH keys, and registry tokens.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Rollup and Vite Build Configuration Files Alter Module Resolution and Code Splitting Logic
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Rollup and Vite build tool configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter module resolution strategies and code splitting configurations, causing frontend build systems to produce bundles with different module boundaries and loading sequences from what developers review in build configuration files.
label: AV-300

title: Credential Mutualization Connects s1ngularity to Shai-Hulud as All Shai-Hulud Patient-Zero Packages Had Nx as Dependency
description: GitGuardian documented credential mutualization connecting the s1ngularity and Shai-Hulud campaigns, noting that all Shai-Hulud patient-zero packages had Nx as a dependency, strongly suggesting that npm tokens stolen from the s1ngularity Nx compromise in August 2025 directly enabled the broader Shai-Hulud worm campaign in September 2025.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Turbopack and Webpack 5 Module Federation Configuration Alter Remote Module Loading URLs
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Turbopack and Webpack 5 Module Federation configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter remote entry point URLs, causing micro-frontend applications to load modules from attacker-controlled hosts instead of legitimate federated module sources invisible during configuration review.
label: AV-300

title: Use Lockfiles Everywhere Never Run pip install or npm install Without Pinned Versions in CI as Primary Supply Chain Defense
description: Security researchers documenting the 2026 supply chain attack wave confirmed that using lockfiles everywhere and never running pip install or npm install without pinned version specifications in CI represents the single most effective immediate defense against supply chain attacks by ensuring builds use specific tested package versions.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Babel Transform Configuration Alter Code Transpilation Rules and Plugin Execution
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Babel JavaScript transpiler configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter transform plugin ordering and configuration parameters, causing Babel to apply different code transformations from what developers intend when reviewing transpilation configuration for security and compatibility requirements.
label: AV-300

title: April 21-23 2026 Coordinated Three-Day Attack Window Hits npm PyPI and Docker Hub Simultaneously Targeting Developer Secrets
description: GitGuardian documented a coordinated three-day attack window from April 21 to 23 2026 where three distinct supply chain attacks simultaneously targeted npm, PyPI, and Docker Hub ecosystems, with all three attacks sharing the primary objective of stealing secrets from developer environments and CI/CD pipelines despite potentially different threat actor groups.
label: AV-410

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in TypeScript tsconfig.json Alter Compiler Options Including strict Mode and Target Environment
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in TypeScript tsconfig.json configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter compiler option settings including strict mode flags, target environment specifications, and module resolution strategies, causing TypeScript projects to be compiled with weaker type checking or different output from what developers expect during configuration review.
label: AV-300
